# AGENTS.md — gMART

Guidance for Codex when working in this repository. Part of the **ICII** workspace
(see `../AGENTS.md` for the system-wide picture). gMART is the **orchestrator** of the system.

> This guide is stored in **ICII** (`Codex/gMART.md`), not inside the repo. The repo itself is a git
> submodule at `../gMART/` (remote `github.com/IDUclub/gMART`); paths below are relative to that submodule root.

## What this is

> *geospatial Multi-Agent Restrictions Test* — exercises LLM ability to extract geospatial
> construction **restrictions** and compute service **provision** from a user prompt, using a fixed
> set of geospatial tools exposed over MCP.

This repo ships **two independently deployable apps** from one codebase:

1. **`src/agents`** — Agents REST API (FastAPI, port 80). LLM-driven agents that plan and run
   tool-call pipelines via Ollama, talking to MCP servers and other IDU services. Also exposes
   **A2A** (agent-to-agent) endpoints and SSE streaming. Agent families: **restriction** extraction,
   **provision** effects, and **document-QA** (RAG over IDU_DVD, `/documents`).
2. **`src/idu_mcp`** — IDU MCP server (FastMCP over HTTP, port 8000). Exposes geometry tools
   (`CreateBuffers`, `CreateRestrictions`) and Urban API tools (`GetServices`, `GetPhysicalObjects`,
   `GetServiceTypeIdByName`) plus restriction prompt templates. Self-documenting via `fastmcp-docs`.

They are built from separate Dockerfiles (`docker/Dockerfile-agents`, `docker/Dockerfile-mcp`) and
run as separate containers in `docker-compose.yaml`.

## Tech stack

- Python **3.11**, managed with **uv** (`uv.lock`). One `pyproject.toml` covers both apps.
- **FastAPI** (agents) + **FastMCP** (idu_mcp, and as a client in agents).
- **Ollama** (`ollama` client) — LLM inference + tool calling.
- **python-a2a** — agent-to-agent protocol; **Redis** — pipeline state + pub/sub for SSE.
- **geopandas**, **shapely**, **geojson-pydantic** — geometry tools.
- `loguru`, `aiohttp`, `pydantic`, `starlette`, `fastmcp-docs`.

## Layout

```
src/
  agents/                         # ── App 1: Agents REST API (port 80) ──
    main.py                       #   FastAPI app + router wiring (dev: agents_dev_runner.py)
    routers/                      #   llm (/llm), restrictions (/restrictions), provision (/provision),
                                  #   documents (/documents — RAG QA), token refresh (/pipelines), system (/system), + A2A controllers
    services/                     #   the orchestration brains:
      restriction_parser_service.py / restriction_*  # restriction extraction (catalog, context, tool_executor)
      provsion_service.py / provision_* (sic)        # provision pipeline (context, plan_builder, tool_executor)
      dvd_rag_service.py / dvd_reasoning.py / dvd_context.py  # document-QA RAG pipeline (IDU_DVD)
      simple_llm_service.py, base_llm_service.py      # raw LLM passthrough
      pipeline_state.py                               # Redis-backed pipeline state
      a2a_service.py, provision_a2a_service.py, dvd_a2a_service.py  # A2A orchestration
    mcp_clients/                  #   outbound MCP clients (FastMCP Client wrappers):
      idu_mcp_client.py           #     -> gMART idu_mcp (this repo's other app)
      effects_mcp_client.py       #     -> ObjectEffectsAPI MCP
      dvd_mcp_client.py           #     -> IDU_DVD MCP (regulatory-docs search; unauthenticated)
      base_mcp_client.py          #     tool listing (ollama format) + execution + token-expiry handling
    model_clients/base_client.py  #   Ollama client wrapper
    api_clients/                  #   chat_storage_client (-> ChatStorage), urban_api_client (-> Urban API)
    a2a/                          #   python-a2a agents, executors, task store
    common/config/app_config.py   #   AgentsAppConfig — the service URL contract (see below)
    dependencies/, dto/, schema/, common/  # DI, models, middleware, auth, exceptions, SSE executors

  idu_mcp/                        # ── App 2: IDU MCP server (port 8000) ──
    main.py                       #   builds main_mcp, mounts sub-servers, FastMCPDocs, /health /logs
    mcp_dev_runner.py             #   local entrypoint
    tools_interfaces/             #   MCP tool definitions (the public tool surface):
      geom_interface.py           #     CreateBuffers, CreateRestrictions  (tags: geometry/*)
      urb_api_interface.py        #     GetServices, GetPhysicalObjects, GetServiceTypeIdByName (tags: urban_api/data)
    tools_services/               #   implementations: geometry_tools, geometry_validator, urb_api_tools
    prompts/restriction_prompts.py#   MCP prompt templates
    tools_descriptions/           #   long tool descriptions + validation messages (Russian, domain-facing)
    api_clients/urban_api_client.py
    dependencies/tool_deps/       #   per-tool dependency injection (geom, urban_api, server)
    common/                       #   config (MCP_*), auth (token_verifier), logging, middleware
```

## Commands

```bash
uv sync                                                  # install deps (both apps + dev group)

# Run the IDU MCP server (port 8000) — start this first; agents depend on it
uv run python -m src.idu_mcp.mcp_dev_runner             # or: uvicorn src.idu_mcp.main:mcp_app --port 8000

# Run the Agents API (port 80)
uv run python -m src.agents.agents_dev_runner           # or: uvicorn src.agents.main:app --port 80

docker compose up -d --build                            # agents + idu_mcp + redis (pipeline_storage)
uv run pre-commit run --all-files                       # lint/format (black + isort)
```

- Agents Swagger: `http://localhost:80/docs` · IDU MCP docs: `http://localhost:8000/docs` (FastMCPDocs),
  health at `/health`, MCP endpoint at `/mcp`.
- **Tests** (`pytest`): `tests/unit` (hermetic — mock every boundary) and `tests/integration`
  (live stack: Redis/Ollama/IDU_DVD MCP, each self-skips when down). Run `uv run pytest tests/unit`.
  `dev/` Dockerfiles (`docker/dev/`) build with `pip install --system`.
- **CI deploy**: `.github/workflows/build_and_deploy_all.yaml` (manual `workflow_dispatch`) builds both
  images and restarts them via `docker-compose.actions.yaml`. Since 0.13.2 the idu_mcp container is
  configured from **GitHub environment variables** (no env file copied from the runner), the compose file
  healthchecks idu_mcp, and `DVD_MCP_SERVER` is forwarded into the agents container.

## Configuration

Two separate env files (examples in `env/`, production-style under `env/development/`):

**`env/.env.agents.example`** → `AgentsAppConfig` (`src/agents/common/config/app_config.py`):

| Variable | Points at |
|---|---|
| `OLLAMA_API_URL` | Ollama LLM server |
| `IDU_MCP_SERVER` | gMART **idu_mcp** (`http://host:8000/mcp`) |
| `OBJECTS_EFFECTS_MCP_SERVER` | **ObjectEffectsAPI** MCP (`http://host:8080/mcp`) |
| `DVD_MCP_SERVER` | **IDU_DVD** MCP for the document-QA agent (`http://host:8000/mcp`) — **optional** |
| `CHAT_STORAGE` | **ChatStorage** service (`http://host:8010`) |
| `URBAN_API_URL` | external Urban API |
| `REDIS_URL` | Redis (pipeline state + SSE pub/sub) |
| `SYSTEM_PASSWORD` | guards `/system` config retrieval |

**`env/.env.idu_mcp.example`** → MCP config (`src/idu_mcp/common/config/mcp_config.py`):

| Variable | Points at |
|---|---|
| `URBAN_API_URL` | external Urban API (for the urban_api tools) |

`docker-compose.yaml` uses the **external** `localnet` network and brings up `idu_agents`,
`idumcp`, and `pipeline_storage` (Redis); agents `depends_on` a healthy idu_mcp + redis.

## Agent flow (how a request is served)

1. A router (e.g. `/restrictions`, `/provision`) hands off to a `*_service`.
2. The service loads available tools from MCP servers via the `mcp_clients` (converted to Ollama
   tool-call format by `base_mcp_client.load_ollama_tools`).
3. The LLM (Ollama) plans tool calls; the service executes them through the MCP clients, threading
   intermediate GeoJSON layers forward via tool-call **meta**.
4. State is persisted in Redis (`pipeline_state`); progress can stream to the client over SSE.
5. The auth token from the caller is forwarded to MCP servers / Urban API (token-expiry surfaces as
   `TokenExpiredError`; refresh via `/pipelines`).

## Conventions

- **Formatting is enforced**: `black` + `isort --profile black`, Python 3.11. Run pre-commit before committing.
- Keep the two apps **decoupled**: `src/agents` and `src/idu_mcp` each have their own `common/`,
  `config/`, `dependencies/`, and entrypoint. Don't cross-import between them — they ship as separate images.
- MCP tool descriptions are written in **Russian** (domain language for urban planners) and are
  detailed/structured — match that style and keep input/output examples accurate, since the LLM relies on them.
- Geometry crosses boundaries as **GeoJSON `FeatureCollection`**; Urban API tools return WGS84 (EPSG:4326).
- Tool names are **stable contract** (`CreateBuffers`, `CreateRestrictions`, `GetServices`, …) — other
  services (ChatStorage replay) special-case them by name. Renaming requires cross-repo coordination.
- **Errors are mapped per interface, never swallowed.** `JsonApiHandler` (both apps) does a *bounded*
  retry (default 3 + exponential backoff) for transient failures only (network errors, 500 "reset by
  peer") — never unbounded recursion. Unhandled statuses raise: in **agents** (REST) an
  `AgentsBaseException` subclass (`AgentsNotFound` 404, `AgentsInputException` 400,
  `DownstreamServiceError` 502) caught by `ExceptionHandlerMiddleware`; in **idu_mcp** a
  `fastmcp.exceptions.ToolError`. Ollama "model not found" (404) is mapped to `ModelNotFound` (404).

## Gotchas

- The provision service/files are spelled **`provsion`** in several places (`provsion_service.py`).
  Match existing spelling for imports; grep both `provsion` and `provision`.
- Two apps, two ports: idu_mcp = **8000**, agents = **80**. The agents env expects idu_mcp reachable at
  `:8000/mcp`. Start idu_mcp first (compose enforces this via healthcheck).
- `idu_mcp/main.py` runs `asyncio.run(docs.setup())` at import time and reconfigures stdout/stderr to
  UTF-8 for Windows consoles — keep that ordering if editing startup (docs must register before
  `http_app()`; the explanatory comment was removed from the code, the constraint still holds).
- `http_app()` is built with **`host_origin_protection=False`** (since 0.13.3, "removed dns filters").
  FastMCP's DNS-rebinding protection rejects requests whose `Host` isn't localhost — with it on, the MCP
  endpoint is unreachable via Docker-network hostnames (`idumcp`, `host.docker.internal`). Keep it off.
- README "Project structure" / "Documentation" sections are stubs; `docs/frontend-service.md` is the
  frontend integration guide.

## Related services

- **Depends on:** gMART idu_mcp, ObjectEffectsAPI MCP, **IDU_DVD MCP** (document-QA agent), ChatStorage, Ollama, Redis, external Urban API.
- This is the entrypoint the frontend talks to (see `docs/frontend-service.md`; document-QA agent: `docs/frontend-document-qa.md`).
